## [00:00 - 03:04]

This is the pubcast shot. I'm John Loomer Stop searching for the magical ad strategy. Let's go So I often hear from Advertisers who are stuck so whether they've booked a one-on-one with me Are they share their roadblocks in my private community? Make sure you join PHC elite by the way, or maybe it's like on social media reddit is a disaster by the way Lots of this happens there. The story goes like this. They've tried everything and they just can't get good results They'll then go through a long list of strategies that they've tried right so oh we did the advantage plus shopping We tried catalog ads look like audiences detailed targeting remarketing to have this intricate testing strategy to help isolate the most clickable ads or They've tried various guru strategies that mm-hmm Guarantee results they sound impressive because they're complicated and these gurus and their students Share their amazing results so we know that it works Yeah, so when someone comes to me with this information Sorry, I just want to shake them a little bit and tell them to wake up It just pains me when I hear this now the problem is rooted in an industry-wide misunderstanding of what impacts results Those gurus and wannabe gurus have somehow convinced a very large number of people That some magical strategy is the secret it's not Most of these claims are nothing but snake oil They ignore the fundamental sources of performance that have nothing to do with a magical strategy They can be packaged and sold in a training course And if anything many of these strategies will actually hurt results Rather than help so let me explain First let's talk about those targeting strategies most of them are pointless back in the day every advertiser Myself included was after the ideal targeting strategy They experimented with different interests and lookalikes They layered interests and lookalikes they create several ad sets targeting each approach Looking to isolate what works best could be a split test. Maybe not didn't matter now for whatever reason Some advertisers still do this now regardless of what you think about algorithmic targeting you can't avoid it now So when you're optimizing for conversions and you use interests and behaviors They're only seen as suggestions The same goes for lookalike audiences and it does not matter if you're using advantage plus audience or you're not

## [03:04 - 06:05]

The algorithm is going to do what it wants to do So when you create multiple ad sets attempting to isolate the best approach each Ad set is likely doing the same thing now. You're going to get different results But it's going to be based in randomness It's not due to some perfect combination of interests and lookalikes So don't get lost in targeting strategies based on interests and lookalikes Of course, there's also remarketing and many of these magical strategies are focused there Now these are convincing because they often do show amazing results But there are so many problems when it comes to remarketing It's usually misleading because results are propped up by view through conversions and it's not incremental or Scalable or potentially even necessary. I say that because Remarketing happens naturally now. So all these people you're saying oh, I want to go after my website visitors my email list And people engage with my Facebook posts and this and that it's happening Anyway, and you can prove this to yourself with audience segments Now I could spend an entire episode talking about the pitfalls of centering your entire strategy around remarketing And yeah, I've actually already done that. So make sure you listen to my episode from February on this topic So what about testing strategies? Well, most of those are counterproductive They're pointless of busyness with very little benefit. So it looks like you're really doing something important It might not really be leading too much Create a separate campaign for testing right a separate ad set for each ad variation Sometimes they recommend optimizing for link clicks so you can isolate the highest CTR Which if you didn't know is complete nonsense Since that doesn't mean it will lead to conversions and then you take the winners and you put them into your main campaign It's all just so unnecessary There's no guarantee that the winners you found in testing usually small sample size testing will perform Well in your main campaign and those results like I said are often based in small sample size randomness I've seen some small budget advertisers do all of this complex nonsense To slice and dice results hoping to find a winner and they're just wasting their time and what little budget they have Of course I did an episode on all the issues of small budget advertisers using big budget strategies a few weeks back So make sure you check that out any potential lift you get from most of these testing strategies is Canceled out by the fragmentation that's happening. It's not that testing is bad, right? But there isn't a magical testing strategy that will fix your bad results

## [06:05 - 09:12]

And then there are all these super complex Strategies that honestly, I really don't even get the point. I'm sure you've seen them They're all across the board create a whole bunch of ad sets with like a dollar daily budget I've seen before right there's a reason behind it not really sure or create ten ad sets targeting the same audience to Honestly, I'm not even sure exactly why most complex structure strategies are Based in voodoo and claim to have mysterious powers over the algorithm So basically these whoever came up with these strategies take advantage of advertisers distrust of meta generally and lack of understanding Overall regarding how the algorithm works It's time that you ignore all of the noise not just the suggestions the recommendations the gurus But all the noise that you're creating for yourself So many signals that you're trying to listen to look the primary source of your problem Problems has nothing to do with whether you've implemented a magical strategy. It's the ad copy the creative the product the offer the landing page No magical strategy will fix a broken product No amount of targeting and complex campaign construction is going to fix bad copy and creative So here's the bottom of the glass. Here's what I want you to walk away with today Abandon those complex strategies. I need you to understand that none of this peripheral stuff Matters all that much if at all. So if you're stuck do not get lost in the how of running ads It's not the secret to your success or your failure. So don't get hung up on that instead keep it as simple as possible and Yes, check out my blog post on this at John Lumer comm slash simple Also did a whole podcast episode on it back in March. Go back check it out I know this sounds crazy, but most advertisers again, I'm not talking about a I'm spending thousands of dollars per day And all this kind of stuff the vast majority of advertisers So everybody else who's spending hundreds or less can get away with a single campaign. That doesn't mean I'm saying you should only run a single campaign But you can and it might be better for you with a single ad set Optimized for conversions and a handful of good. Well thought out ads that might not be your long-term approach But it's honestly a great way to figure out what's working in what's not and Understanding what is getting in the way of good results? Because when you've simplified it like that and it doesn't work guess what? It's not because of campaign construction It's not because of a magical strategy that you fail to implement and to be honest when you realize this It's freeing so shift your focus back to your ads your product and your landing page and then ask yourself

Why aren't people converting?
